runs.reasoningSummary: Microeconometric studies historically estimate the intensive-margin Frisch elasticity for prime-age workers (especially men) to be quite low, typically between 0.1 and 0.3 (e.g., MaCurdy 1981, Altonji 1986). (2011), suggest an intensive-margin Frisch elasticity around 0.3 to 0.5 when accounting for optimization frictions. Therefore, a median estimate of 0.3 represents a standard consensus for this specific parameter.

Estimates above 1.0 are generally considered too high for the intensive margin alone."
}
